qt: Intervals in R are always_______.

If the non-empty sets say $A,B$ are measurable then $A-B$__________.
If a set in Euclidean space is translated by a fixed amount in any direction, what happens to its Lebesgue measure?
A set $E$ is a Borel set if it can be expressed as_____________.
op: neither countable union nor intersection of open or closed sets
either countable union or intersection of open or closed sets

What distinguishes the Cantor set from intervals of positive length?
In the first iteration for constructing Cantor Set from $[0,1],$ we drop a sub-interval of length__________.
Which of the following is a property of Borel sets?
op: Borel sets cannot contain uncountably many elements.
The complement of a Borel set is always an open set.

Suppose $f$ is a real valued identically constant function $f(x)=\beta ,$ then $\left\{ x:f(x)>\alpha \right\} =% %TCIMACRO{\U{211d} }% %BeginExpansion \mathbb{R} %EndExpansion$. And if $\beta >\alpha$, then__________.

Let $M$ be a measurable space (a sigma algebra of measurable sets) then a function $f:E\longrightarrow %TCIMACRO{\U{211d} }% %BeginExpansion \mathbb{R} %EndExpansion$ on $\varnothing \neq E\in M$ given as $\left\{ x\in E:f(x)>a\right\}$ is measurable if $\forall a,$----------under $f$ is measurable set.
$${\text{Which of the following best describes a measurable function?}}$$
op: $${\text{A function that maps measurable sets to measurable sets}}{\text{.}}$$
$${\text{A function that can be represented as the union of countably many open sets}}{\text{.}}$$
$${\text{A function that maps Borel sets to Lebesgue measurable sets}}{\text{.}}$$
$${\text{A function that preserves the order of elements in its domain}}{\text{.}}$$

qt: $${\text{Which of the following sets is countable and also a Borel set with measure zero?}}$$
What is a characteristic function in mathematics?
op: A function that describes the characteristics of a set
A function that calculates the average of a set
A function that assigns values to elements of a set
A function that indicates membership of an element in a set

qt: What does the characteristic function XA∩B​(x) represent?
op: The maximum value of the characteristic functions of sets A and B.
The minimum value of the characteristic functions of sets A and B.
The difference of the characteristic functions of sets A and B.
The sum of the characteristic functions of sets A and B.

qt: $${\text{Let }}f:\mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}{\text{ defined by }}f(x) = 2.{\text{ The set }}\{ x \in \mathbb{R}:f(x) > 7\} {\text{ is equal to which of the following sets?}}$$
What value does the characteristic function relative to a set non-emty set A take if an element belongs to the set A?

Let $M$ be a measurable space(a sigma algebra of measurable sets) then a function $f:E\longrightarrow %TCIMACRO{\U{211d} }% %BeginExpansion \mathbb{R} %EndExpansion$ is Lebesgue measurable function on $\varnothing \neq E\in M,$ if_____________.
How does monotonicity in characteristic functions help in comparing sets?
op: It facilitates understanding of the relationship between sets.
It provides insights into the functions' behavior.
It helps in finding the derivative of the functions.
It aids in determining continuity of the functions.

qt: $${\text{Let }}f:\mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}{\text{ defined by }}f(x) = 9.{\text{ The set }}\{ x \in \mathbb{R}:f(x) > 7\} {\text{ is equal to which of the following sets?}}$$
Which of the following statements accurately describes countable sets in relation to Borel sets?
op: Countable sets cannot be Borel sets.
Countable sets are Borel sets but may or may not have measure zero.
Countable sets are always Borel sets with measure zero.
Countable sets are not Borel sets with measure zero.

qt: Let $M$ be a measurable space (a sigma algebra of measurable sets) then a function $f:E\longrightarrow %TCIMACRO{\U{211d} }% %BeginExpansion \mathbb{R} %EndExpansion$ on $\varnothing \neq E\in M$ is measurable if the set $\left\{ x\in E:f(x)>a\right\}$ is measurable for all __________.

If one set is a subset of another, what is the relationship between their characteristic functions?
op: The larger set's characteristic function is less than the smaller set's.
The smaller set's characteristic function is less than or equal to the larger set's.

qt: What does the characteristic function of the empty set indicate?
op: That no element belongs to the set.
That the set contains all possible elements.
That every element belongs to the set.
That the set has infinite elements.

qt: What property describes the characteristic function of the empty set?
What does it mean if the characteristic function of set A is less than the characteristic function of set B for all elements?
op: A is a proper subset of B
A and B are equal sets
A and B are disjoint sets
A and B have the same cardinality

What does monotonicity in characteristic functions refer to?
op: The function's behavior as the input varies.
The function's relationship with set inclusion.
The function's continuity over its domain.

qt: Let x,y\in \left[ 0,1\right) ,then the sum modulo 1;x\widehat{+}y of \ x and \ y is defined by_________.
op: x+y if x+y\leq 1 and x+y-1 if x+y>1
x+y if x+y<1 and x+y-1 if x+y\geq 1
x+y if x+y\geq 1 and x+y-1 if x+y<1
